title: Tell the Exxon Valdez Oil Spill Trustee Council to Stop the Spend-Down
summary: The small city of Seward has always been a fishing town. Our people and economy relied almost entirely on fishing and tourism. At the time of the spill, I sat on the Board as president of the Seward Life Action Council, an agency that managed a number of public health and social service programs. We witnessed how the spill not only caused irreparable damage to the ecosystem and the commercial, sport and subsistence use of our once bountiful ocean resources; but also left devastating mental health issues in its wake.
